title: local image lookup by digest
body:
Detect local-image lookups by digest.  Those clearly refer to local
images only, so we must not proceed to remote lookups.

Fixes: #2836
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

My experiments with `docker build` suggest that it finds an image if the hex portion of the digest specified to a `FROM` instruction matches an image ID, but not if it matches an image's (manifest's) digest.  This form is also recognized by `docker create`, so it may belong in the storage library.

> My experiments with `docker build` suggest that it finds an image if the hex portion of the digest specified to a `FROM` instruction matches an image ID, but not if it matches an image's (manifest's) digest. This form is also recognized by `docker create`, so it may belong in the storage library.

Shall we support both?  We could first assume it's an image ID, and then fall back to the real digests.  Could handle both in the Buildah.

> > My experiments with `docker build` suggest that it finds an image if the hex portion of the digest specified to a `FROM` instruction matches an image ID, but not if it matches an image's (manifest's) digest. This form is also recognized by `docker create`, so it may belong in the storage library.
> 
> Shall we support both?

I'm personally in no hurry to encourage either, and `docker` doesn't support the latter.
Updated to only cover the image ID.
